# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

```typescript
import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.157.0/http/server.ts";
import { openKv, KvObject } from 'https://deno.land/x/kv@v0.2.0/mod.ts';

const kv = await openKv();

async function getUser(userId: string, filterParam?: string) {
  let userKey;
  
  if (filterParam) {
    userKey = ['users', userId, filterParam];
  } else {
    userKey = ['users', userId];
  }
  
  return await kv.get(userKey);
}

const server = serve({ port: 8000 });
console.log("Server is running on localhost:8000");

for await (const req of server) {
  const url = new URL(req.request.url);
  const userId = url.searchParams.get('userId');
  const filterParam = url.searchParams.get('filterParam');
  
  if (userId) {
    const responseBody = await getUser(userId, filterParam);
    
    req.respond({ status: 200, body: JSON.stringify(responseBody)});
  } else {
    req.respond({ status: 400, body: "Missing userId parameter" });
 <｜begin▁of▁sentence｜>}
